Meta.Title: Beijing 1-Day Travel Plan
Meta.Summary: A compact 1-day itinerary covering Beijings core highlights: the Forbidden City area, classic hutongs, and an evening food and nightlife stop. Timed to balance crowds, walking, and transit.

Item.Activity: Arrive at Tiananmen Square (outer viewing) and walk toward the Forbidden City entrance area.
Item.Notes: Bring passport/ID. Security checks are common. Consider pre-booking tickets if required.
Enter early to avoid peak crowds. Focus on main axis halls, then exit via the north gate.

Item.Activity: Jingshan Park for the central overlook of the Forbidden City.
Item.Notes: Short uphill walk; best panoramic photo spot.

Item.Activity: Lunch near Shichahai/Houhai or in a hutong courtyard restaurant.
Item.Notes: Try Beijing zhajiangmian, jianbing, or roast duck if you want a heavier meal.

Item.Activity: Hutong walk: Shichahai, Yandai Xiejie, and nearby lanes (optional rickshaw ride).
Item.Notes: Mix of lakeside views and traditional alleys. Allocate time for tea shops or small museums.

Item.Activity: Temple of Heaven (Tiantan) visit and park stroll.
Item.Notes: Enter from the east gate if convenient. See the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests and surrounding grounds.

Item.Activity: Dinner: Peking duck or local northern cuisine.
Item.Notes: Reserve ahead for popular duck restaurants. Split portions if short on time.

Item.Activity: Evening walk and snacks at Wangfujing area or return to Houhai for lakeside lights.
Item.Notes: Choose Wangfujing for shopping/street food; choose Houhai for a relaxed night stroll.

Item.Activity: Return to hotel and prep for next day.
Item.Notes: Build buffer for subway closing times and taxi availability.

Item.Details: Use the subway for speed. Tiananmen/Forbidden City area has controlled access; follow signed pedestrian routes.
Check current ticketing rules for the Palace Museum and Temple of Heaven; reservations may be required.
Swap the afternoon for a half-day Great Wall visit (Mutianyu recommended) and keep the hutongs for evening.
